methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Lumina Vehicles is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Ahmed Shubber and Federico Di Palma. The valuation universe comprises nine comparable companies: eight private firms including 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics, plus one public equity (ticker 277810.KQ). This peer set spans the robotics and manufacturing sector.

The peer-median method is not applicable in this instance. The analysis identified eleven same-sector peers within Robotics & Manufacturing, but filtering for same-stage, adjacent-stage, or similar-valuation-tier comparables yielded fewer than three datapoints—below the minimum threshold of three required for reliable triangulation. Applying an all-sector median across disparate valuation tiers would introduce material bias and was therefore rejected.

Without a sufficiently dense narrow peer set and absent disclosed financials or transaction multiples for Lumina Vehicles, no quantitative valuation range can be derived from the available data. A defensible valuation narrative would require either (1) expansion of comparable company data with disclosed metrics from the private peer set, (2) transaction or funding round precedents at identical or adjacent stages, or (3) bottom-up cost-of-capital analysis. Key uncertainties include the target's stage-specific burn rate, capital efficiency, and addressable market penetration relative to peer benchmarks.
